attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content:
# Org template config, admin dashboard & in-app config editor ## Context Pawtograder creates per-assignment **handout** and **solution (grader)** repos by generating from two **hardcoded** GitHub template repos: - `assignment-create-handout-repo/index.ts` → `TEMPLATE_HANDOUT_REPO_NAME = "pawtograder/template-assignment-handout"` - `assignment-create-solution-repo/index.ts` → `TEMPLATE_SOLUTION_REPO_NAME = "pawtograder/template-assignment-grader"` Institutions that run their own GitHub org need their own templates. Today `github_org` is just a `text` column on `classes`; there is **no `github_orgs` table** and no way to configure templates per org or per class. Separately, the admin portal (`app/admin/*`) can create/list/archive classes but gives admins no way to *operate inside* a course or inspect per-org GitHub config, and there is no in-app way to edit the GitHub Actions workflow files / `pawtograder.yml` that live in those repos (only a read-only `repository-get-file` function exists). This delivers four phases in one effort: - **A.** Org-level handout/solution template defaults, overridable per class. - **B.** Admin per-org dashboard showing each course's effective config. - **C.** A Monaco-based editor for workflow & `pawtograder.yml` files with live validation that **commits back** to the repo. - **D.** An admin dashboard course-context switcher to "act as an instructor". Plus a rich set of Playwright e2e tests across all four. ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph DB["Phase A — data + resolution"] GO["new table github_orgs
(default handout/solution template)"] CL["classes +handout_template_repo
+solution_template_repo (override)"] RPC["resolve_class_template_repos(class_id)
override → org default → constant"] GO --> RPC CL --> RPC end subgraph EF["edge functions"] EH["assignment-create-handout-repo"] ES["assignment-create-solution-repo"] WF["new: repository-write-file
(GitHubWrapper.writeFileToRepo)"] end RPC --> EH RPC --> ES subgraph FE["frontend"] B["Phase B
/admin/github-orgs[/[org]]"] C["Phase C
RepoFileEditor (monaco-yaml)"] D["Phase D
/admin course switcher
+ admin-viewing banner"] end GO --> B RPC --> B WF --> C EH -.template editing.-> C D --> CTX["ClassProfileProvider:
admin → provisioned instructor role"] ``` --- ## Phase A — Org & class template configuration ### Migration (`supabase/migrations/_github_org_templates.sql`) 1. **`github_orgs` table** keyed by org name (matches `classes.github_org`): - `org_name text primary key` - `default_handout_template_repo text not null default 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'` - `default_solution_template_repo text not null default 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'` - `created_at`, `updated_at` timestamptz, `created_by`, `updated_by` uuid → `auth.users`. - RLS: admins (global `user_roles.role='admin'`) full CRUD; instructors of any class in that org may `SELECT` (use the existing `authorizeforclassinstructor` pattern / an `EXISTS` against `classes` + `user_roles`). Follow the RLS style in `20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql`. 2. **Class override columns** on `classes`: - `handout_template_repo text null`, `solution_template_repo text null` (NULL = inherit org default). No backfill needed. 3. **Resolution RPC** `resolve_class_template_repos(p_class_id bigint)` returning `(handout_template_repo text, solution_template_repo text)`: `COALESCE(class override, github_orgs default, hardcoded constant)`. `SECURITY DEFINER`, guarded by `authorizeforclassinstructor(p_class_id)`. 4. **Admin RPCs** (mirror `admin_get_classes` / `admin_create_class` in the admin-portal migration; all guard on global admin role): - `admin_get_github_orgs()` → one row per distinct org (union of `github_orgs` and orgs referenced by `classes.github_org`), with defaults and a `course_count`. - `admin_upsert_github_org(p_org_name text, p_handout text, p_solution text)`. - `admin_get_org_courses(p_org_name text)` → courses in the org with their override columns + resolved effective templates (call the resolution logic). After writing the migration, run `npm run client-local` to regenerate `utils/supabase/SupabaseTypes.d.ts` (auto-copied to `_shared/`). **Do not hand-edit it.** ### Edge functions — use resolved templates instead of constants In `assignment-create-handout-repo/index.ts` and `assignment-create-solution-repo/index.ts`, replace the hardcoded `TEMPLATE_*_REPO_NAME` passed to `createRepo(org, name, template, …)` with a value resolved via the new RPC. Add a shared helper, e.g. `resolveTemplateRepos(supabase, classId)` in `supabase/functions/_shared/GitHubSyncHelpers.ts`, that calls `supabase.rpc("resolve_class_template_repos", { p_class_id })` and returns `{ handout, solution }`, falling back to the existing constants if the row is missing. Keep the constants as the documented fallback default. --- ## Phase B — Admin per-org dashboard - **Nav:** add a "GitHub Orgs" `Link` in `app/admin/layout.tsx` (alongside Classes/Import/…). - **List page** `app/admin/github-orgs/page.tsx` (+ `layout.tsx` metadata): server component calling `admin_get_github_orgs()`; renders a Chakra `Table` (mirror `app/admin/classes/ClassManagementTable.tsx`) of orgs with course counts and the two default template repos, each row linking to the detail page. - **Detail page** `app/admin/github-orgs/[org]/page.tsx`: an editor for the org's default handout/solution template repos (client component using `supabase.rpc("admin_upsert_github_org", …)`, `toaster` for feedback — same pattern as `app/admin/signup-welcome/page.tsx`), plus a table from `admin_get_org_courses(org)` listing each course, whether it overrides or inherits each template, the effective value, and a "Manage as instructor" link (Phase D). Embed the Phase C `RepoFileEditor` here so admins can edit the org's template repo files directly. --- ## Phase C — Monaco config-file editor with commit-back ### Write path (edge function + wrapper) 1. **`GitHubWrapper.writeFileToRepo(repoName, path, content, message, sha?, scope?)`** in `supabase/functions/_shared/GitHubWrapper.ts`: `PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}` with base64-encoded content and `sha` for updates (omit for create). Mirror the octokit + error handling style of `getFileFromRepo` (lines ~640–667). 2. **New edge function `supabase/functions/repository-write-file/index.ts`**: copy `repository-get-file/index.ts` structure exactly — `assertUserIsInstructor(courseId, …)`, the same `github_org` ownership check (request org must equal the course's `github_org`, or be `pawtograder`), and rate limiting. Body: `{ courseId, orgName, repoName, path, content, message, sha }`. Add `WriteFileRequest` to `_shared/FunctionTypes.d.ts` (mirror `GetFileRequest`) and a `repositoryWriteFile(...)` wrapper in `lib/edgeFunctions.ts` (mirror `repositoryGetFile`, line ~62). ### Editor component `components/github/RepoFileEditor.tsx` - Reuse `@monaco-editor/react` + `monaco-yaml` (already deps) following the setup in `lib/gradebookExpressionMonaco.ts`. - Props: `{ courseId, orgName, repoName, path }`. Load via `repositoryGetFile` (returns `{ content, sha }`), edit in Monaco, save via `repositoryWriteFile` passing the loaded `sha` (optimistic concurrency; on 409 re-fetch and warn). - **Live validation/autocomplete via monaco-yaml `schemas`**, matched by file path: - `pawtograder.yml` → a JSON Schema derived from `PawtograderConfig` (`supabase/functions/_shared/PawtograderYml.d.ts` / `utils/PawtograderYml`). Reuse the existing structural checks in `components/ui/autograder-configuration.tsx` (`validatePawtograderConfig`) for a save-time guard. - `.github/workflows/*.{yml,yaml}` → the public GitHub Actions workflow JSON Schema (bundle a local copy under `lib/schemas/` to avoid a network fetch at runtime; cite SchemaStore in a comment). - Optional file picker: list candidate files with the existing `repositoryListFiles` wrapper, filtered to `pawtograder.yml` and `.github/workflows/*`. ### Where it's surfaced - **Instructor:** a new course-settings page (below) and the assignment **autograder** page (`app/course/[course_id]/manage/assignments/[assignment_id]/autograder/page.tsx`), which already reads `pawtograder.yml` — add an "Edit config files" panel mounting `RepoFileEditor` against the solution `grader_repo`. - **Course-level GitHub settings page** `app/course/[course_id]/manage/course/github/page.tsx`: lets instructors set the class-level `handout_template_repo` / `solution_template_repo` overrides (plain `useUpdate` on `classes`, refine pattern as in the autograder page) and shows the effective resolved templates. Add a "GitHub Templates" entry under "Course Settings" in `app/course/[course_id]/dynamicCourseNav.tsx` (instructors_only). - **Admin:** mounted in the Phase B org detail page for the org's template repos. --- ## Phase D — Admin "act as instructor" course switcher Admins already pass DB instructor authorization (`authorizeforclassinstructor` and `assertUserIsInstructor` return true for `role='admin'`), but `ClassProfileProvider` (`hooks/useClassProfiles.tsx`) shows "You don't have access to this course" when the admin has no `user_roles` row for that class, and instructor UI gates on `role.role==='instructor'`. **Approach (reliable, reuses the entire instructor surface):** idempotently provision the admin a real instructor enrollment in the target class on entry. 1. **RPC `admin_enter_course_as_instructor(p_class_id bigint)`** (`SECURITY DEFINER`, guard global admin role): if the admin has no active `user_roles` row for `p_class_id`, create the required `profiles` (private + public) and an `instructor` `user_roles` row for them (follow profile/role creation already done in `admin_create_class` and the enrollment helpers). Idempotent. Audit-log via the existing audit mechanism used by other admin RPCs. 2. **Admin dashboard:** in `app/admin/AdminDashboardContent.tsx` (and the Phase B org-course table), add a **"Manage as instructor"** action per course that calls the RPC then navigates to `/course/[id]/manage`. Surface a course picker on `/admin` for quick access. 3. **Admin-viewing banner:** add a small client banner shown in the course manage area when the current user holds a global admin role (detect via `allOfMyRoles` from `useClassProfiles` / a lightweight `useIsAdmin` check). Mount it in `app/course/[course_id]/manage/loading.tsx`'s sibling — i.e. add it to the manage tree; if no manage `layout.tsx` exists, create `app/course/[course_id]/manage/layout.tsx` that renders the banner above `children`. > Decision note for the implementer: provisioning a real instructor row is chosen over > synthesizing a fake role because profile-dependent instructor actions (posting, > assignments, audit author) would otherwise break. If polluting staff rosters is a > concern, gate these rows out of staff listings with an `EXISTS` admin check rather than > changing this approach. --- ## End-to-end tests (Playwright, `tests/e2e/`) Use `tests/e2e/TestingUtils.ts` helpers (`createClass`, `loginAsUser`, `role: "admin"` support, magic-link login). Follow the structure of existing specs such as `course-feature-dashboard.test.tsx` and `enrollments.test.tsx`. - **`github-org-templates.test.tsx`** — Phase A/B: - Admin sets org default handout/solution templates via `/admin/github-orgs/[org]`; `admin_get_github_orgs` reflects them. - Class override on the course GitHub settings page takes precedence; assert `resolve_class_template_repos` returns override > org default > constant for three seeded states (override set / org default only / neither). - Instructor without admin cannot see `/admin/github-orgs` (redirect), confirming RLS. - **`repo-file-editor.test.tsx`** — Phase C: - Load `pawtograder.yml` into `RepoFileEditor`, introduce invalid YAML/schema → live error shown and save blocked; fix → save calls `repositoryWriteFile`. - Mock/stub GitHub at the edge-function boundary (the e2e GitHub mock used elsewhere — check `pawtograder-playground`/`END_TO_END_REPO_PREFIX` handling) so writes don't hit real GitHub; assert the write request payload (path, message, sha) is well-formed. - Workflow-file path loads the Actions schema and reports a known invalid key. - **`admin-act-as-instructor.test.tsx`** — Phase D: - Admin (not enrolled) clicks "Manage as instructor", lands on `/course/[id]/manage` successfully (no "no access" screen), sees the admin-viewing banner, and can load an instructor-only page (e.g. gradebook). Assert `admin_enter_course_as_instructor` is idempotent on repeat entry. - Non-admin hitting `/admin/*` is redirected (existing admin layout guard). --- ## Verification 1. `npx supabase db reset` (apply migration + seed), then `npm run client-local` and `npx tsc --noEmit` / `npm run typecheck:functions` clean. 2. `npm run lint` clean on changed files. 3. Run the new specs: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/github-org-templates.test.tsx tests/e2e/repo-file-editor.test.tsx tests/e2e/admin-act-as-instructor.test.tsx`, plus the existing admin/enrollment specs to catch regressions. 4. Manual smoke (local Supabase, instructor email contains "instructor"; admin seeded via `role:"admin"`): set an org default, override on a class, confirm a newly created handout/solution repo uses the resolved template; edit a `pawtograder.yml` in the editor and confirm the commit; enter a course from `/admin` as instructor and back out. ## Files (representative) - New: migration `…_github_org_templates.sql`; `supabase/functions/repository-write-file/index.ts`; `app/admin/github-orgs/{page,layout}.tsx`, `app/admin/github-orgs/[org]/page.tsx`; `app/course/[course_id]/manage/course/github/page.tsx`; `app/course/[course_id]/manage/layout.tsx`; `components/github/RepoFileEditor.tsx`; `lib/schemas/github-workflow.schema.json`; three e2e specs. - Edit: `supabase/functions/_shared/GitHubWrapper.ts` (`writeFileToRepo`), `_shared/GitHubSyncHelpers.ts` (`resolveTemplateRepos`), `_shared/FunctionTypes.d.ts`; `assignment-create-handout-repo/index.ts`, `assignment-create-solution-repo/index.ts`; `lib/edgeFunctions.ts`; `app/admin/layout.tsx`; `app/admin/AdminDashboardContent.tsx`; `app/course/[course_id]/dynamicCourseNav.tsx`; the assignment `autograder/page.tsx`.

I'm implementing a new feature in this Next.js + Supabase codebase (repo root /scratch/platform). I need you to map the existing patterns for SQL migrations and admin RPCs so I can mirror them exactly.

Investigate and report (with file paths, line numbers, and verbatim code excerpts):

1. The migration `supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` — full structure: how the github_orgs-style tables would be styled, RLS policy style, how admin role is checked (global user_roles.role='admin'), and the existing admin RPCs `admin_get_classes`, `admin_create_class`. Quote the full SQL of those RPCs and their RLS/grant patterns.

2. The `authorizeforclassinstructor` SQL function — where it's defined and its exact signature/body.

3. How `classes` table is defined (find the migration that creates it) — show the `github_org` column and surrounding columns.

4. The audit-log mechanism used by admin RPCs (the plan mentions "the existing audit mechanism used by other admin RPCs") — find it and show how it's called from within an admin RPC.

5. How profiles + user_roles rows are created for enrollment (look at admin_create_class and any enrollment helper RPCs) — I need to replicate creating a private+public profile and an instructor user_roles row.

6. The naming/timestamp convention for migration filenames (list the most recent ~10 migration filenames).

Report concrete, copy-pasteable details. Do not modify any files.

Quote the relevant sections.

2. `supabase/functions/assignment-create-solution-repo/index.ts` — same, for `TEMPLATE_SOLUTION_REPO_NAME = "pawtograder/template-assignment-grader"`.

3. `supabase/functions/repository-get-file/index.ts` — full structure: how `assertUserIsInstructor(courseId, …)` is used, the `github_org` ownership check (request org must equal course's github_org or be "pawtograder"), rate limiting, request body shape, response shape `{ content, sha }`. Quote it.

4. `supabase/functions/_shared/GitHubWrapper.ts` — the `getFileFromRepo` method (around lines 640-667) verbatim, plus the class/octokit setup and how `createRepo` works, and the constructor / how a GitHubWrapper instance is obtained.

5. `supabase/functions/_shared/GitHubSyncHelpers.ts` — overview of what's exported, and how it builds a supabase client (so I can add a `resolveTemplateRepos(supabase, classId)` helper).

input.prompt:
I'm implementing a Monaco-based config editor and an admin "act as instructor" feature in this Next.js + Supabase codebase (repo root /scratch/platform). Map existing patterns. Report file paths, line numbers, and verbatim excerpts.

1. `lib/gradebookExpressionMonaco.ts` — how @monaco-editor/react and monaco are set up, how the editor `beforeMount`/`onMount` and language config are wired. Quote the setup. Also confirm `monaco-yaml` and `@monaco-editor/react` are in package.json dependencies (quote the relevant lines).

2. `components/ui/autograder-configuration.tsx` — the `validatePawtograderConfig` function (verbatim) and how it's used.

3. `supabase/functions/_shared/PawtograderYml.d.ts` and/or `utils/PawtograderYml*` — the `PawtograderConfig` type definition (verbatim or summarized with all fields), so I can derive a JSON Schema.

4. `lib/edgeFunctions.ts` — the `repositoryGetFile` wrapper (around line 62) and `repositoryListFiles` wrapper verbatim, and the general pattern for these wrappers (how they call supabase.functions.invoke).

5. `hooks/useClassProfiles.tsx` — the `ClassProfileProvider` component: where it shows "You don't have access to this course", the `allOfMyRoles` export, the role shape (`role.role==='instructor'`), and how I'd detect a global admin. Quote the relevant parts.

6. How instructor-only UI gating works (search for `role.role === 'instructor'` usage and any `useIsAdmin`-like hook). 

Do not modify any files. Be concrete and copy-pasteable.

I'm writing new Playwright e2e tests in this codebase (repo root /scratch/platform, tests under tests/e2e/). Map the existing patterns so my new specs match. Report file paths, line numbers, and verbatim excerpts.

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Migration & RPC Pattern Analysis Report

I've completed a thorough investigation of the existing migration patterns and admin RPC architecture. Here are the concrete patterns you need to replicate:

---

### 1. **Migration Naming Convention (Recent 10)**

File: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/`

Timestamps follow `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` format:
- `20260524000944_create_help_request_with_participants_rpc.sql`
- `20260522120002_invitation_enrollment_functions.sql`
- `20260522120001_dedupe_enrollment_profiles.sql`
- `20260522120000_invitations_remove_expiry_rename_dropped.sql`
- `20260521173000_enable_realtime_for_classes.sql`
- `20260520140000_update_rubric_full_foreign_ids.sql`
- `20260520130000_regrade-unapplied-checks-and-dangling.sql`
- `20260520120000_grader_can_update_own_comment_no_review_assignment.sql`
- `20260519120000_update_rubric_full.sql`
- `20260518203000_recalculate_gradebook_on_submission_active_change.sql`

**Pattern**: Use `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (e.g., `20260525143000`) for new migrations.

---

### 2. **Admin Portal System Migration: Full Structure**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` (Lines 1-1270)

#### **Authorization Pattern** (Lines 4-20)

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION authorize_for_admin(p_user_id uuid DEFAULT auth.uid())
RETURNS boolean AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Allow service role (for edge functions)
    IF auth.role() = 'service_role' THEN
        RETURN true;
    END IF;
    
    -- Check if user has admin role in any class
    RETURN EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur
        WHERE ur.user_id = p_user_id 
          AND ur.role = 'admin'
          AND ur.disabled = false
    );
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY INVOKER;
```

**Key Pattern**: 
- Global admin check: looks across ANY class for `role = 'admin'`
- Respects `disabled = false` status
- Service role bypass for edge functions

---

### 3. **admin_create_class RPC** (Lines 23-119)

**Signature & Full SQL**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION admin_create_class(
    p_name text,
    p_term integer,
    p_description text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_github_org_name text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_github_template_prefix text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_created_by uuid DEFAULT auth.uid(),
    p_course_title text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_start_date date DEFAULT NULL,
    p_end_date date DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS bigint AS $$
DECLARE
    v_class_id bigint;
    v_public_profile_id uuid;
    v_private_profile_id uuid;
BEGIN
    SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public;
    
    -- Check admin authorization
    IF NOT authorize_for_admin(p_created_by) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    -- Validate required fields
    IF p_name IS NULL OR trim(p_name) = '' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Class name is required';
    END IF;
    
    IF p_term IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Term is required';
    END IF;

    -- Insert new class
    INSERT INTO public.classes (
        name,
        term,
        description,
        github_org,
        slug,
        course_title,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        created_at
    ) VALUES (
        trim(p_name),
        p_term,
        p_description,
        p_github_org_name,
        p_github_template_prefix,
        p_course_title,
        p_start_date,
        p_end_date,
        now()
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_class_id;

    -- Create admin user role for the creator
    -- Create public profile
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (
        name,
        class_id,
        is_private_profile
    ) VALUES (
        (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
        v_class_id,
        false
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_public_profile_id;

    -- Create private profile
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (
        name,
        class_id,
        is_private_profile
    ) VALUES (
        (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
        v_class_id,
        true
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_private_profile_id;

    -- Create admin user role
    INSERT INTO public.user_roles (
        user_id,
        class_id,
        role,
        public_profile_id,
        private_profile_id
    ) VALUES (
        p_created_by,
        v_class_id,
        'admin',
        v_public_profile_id,
        v_private_profile_id
    );

    RETURN v_class_id;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

**Critical Patterns**:
- `SECURITY DEFINER` for privilege escalation
- `SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public` for isolation
- Authorization check FIRST before any mutations
- Create TWO profiles: one public (`is_private_profile = false`), one private (`is_private_profile = true`)
- Both profiles have same `name` and `class_id`
- User role links to both profiles via `public_profile_id` and `private_profile_id`

---

### 4. **admin_get_classes RPC** (Lines 193-241)

**Full SQL**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION admin_get_classes()
RETURNS TABLE (
    id bigint,
    name text,
    term integer,
    description text,
    github_org_name text,
    github_template_prefix text,
    created_at timestamptz,
    student_count bigint,
    instructor_count bigint,
    archived boolean
) AS $$
BEGIN
    SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public;
    
    -- Check admin authorization
    IF NOT authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT 
        c.id,
        c.name,
        c.term,
        c.description,
        c.github_org as github_org_name,
        c.slug as github_template_prefix,
        c.created_at,
        COALESCE(student_counts.student_count, 0) as student_count,
        COALESCE(instructor_counts.instructor_count, 0) as instructor_count,
        COALESCE(c.archived, false) as archived
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT class_id, COUNT(*) as student_count
        FROM public.user_roles
        WHERE role = 'student'
        GROUP BY class_id
    ) student_counts ON c.id = student_counts.class_id
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT class_id, COUNT(*) as instructor_count
        FROM public.user_roles
        WHERE role = 'instructor'
        GROUP BY class_id
    ) instructor_counts ON c.id = instructor_counts.class_id
    ORDER BY c.created_at DESC;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

---

### 5. **Classes Table Definition** 

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250330003141_remote_schema.sql`

**Structure**:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "public"."classes" (
    "id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "created_at" timestamp with time zone DEFAULT "now"() NOT NULL,
    "name" "text",
    "semester" smallint,
    "canvas_id" integer,
    "time_zone" "text",
    "slug" "text",                    -- github_template_prefix
    "is_demo" boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    "github_org" "text"               -- github_org_name
);
```

**Later columns added** (in admin_portal_system.sql, lines 598-636):
- `archived boolean DEFAULT false`
- `description text`
- `course_title text`
- `term integer` (renamed from `semester`)
- `start_date date`
- `end_date date`

**Mapping for your admin_create_class call**:
- `p_github_org_name` → `github_org` column
- `p_github_template_prefix` → `slug` column

---

### 6. **authorizeforclassinstructor Function**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` (Lines 742-770)

**Full Implementation**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION "public"."authorizeforclassinstructor"("class__id" bigint) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE "plpgsql" STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    SET "search_path" TO ''
    AS $$
begin
    RAISE NOTICE 'authorizeforclassinstructor called with class_id: %, user_id: %', class__id, auth.uid();
    -- First check if user is an admin anywhere - admins have global access
    if exists (
        select 1
        from public.user_roles as r
        where r.user_id = auth.uid() 
          and r.role = 'admin' 
          and r.disabled = false
    ) then
        return true;
    end if;

    -- Otherwise, check for instructor role in this specific class
    return exists (
        select 1
        from public.user_roles as r
        where r.class_id = class__id 
          and r.user_id = auth.uid() 
          and r.role = 'instructor'
          and r.disabled = false
    );
end;
$$;
```

**Key Points**:
- Admins bypass class-specific checks (have global access)
- Checks both admin AND class-specific instructor roles
- Respects `disabled = false` flag
- Uses `SECURITY DEFINER` with `SET search_path = ''` for isolation

---

### 7. **Audit Log Mechanism**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20251228143943_partitioned_audit_system.sql` (Lines 156-201)

**Statement-Level Trigger Function**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.audit_statement_trigger()
RETURNS trigger
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = pg_catalog, public, auth
AS $$
DECLARE
    remote_ip text;
    current_user_id uuid;
BEGIN
    -- Get current user ID
    current_user_id := auth.uid();
    
    -- Extract IP address from request headers
    SELECT split_part(
        current_setting('request.headers', true)::json->>'x-forwarded-for',
        ',', 1) INTO remote_ip;
        
    CASE TG_OP
    WHEN 'INSERT' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all new rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT n.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, NULL, row_to_json(n), remote_ip
        FROM NEW_TABLE n;
        
    WHEN 'UPDATE' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all updated rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT COALESCE(n.class_id, o.class_id), current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, 
               row_to_json(o), row_to_json(n), remote_ip
        FROM NEW_TABLE n
        JOIN OLD_TABLE o ON n.id = o.id;
        
    WHEN 'DELETE' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all deleted rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT o.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, row_to_json(o), NULL, remote_ip
        FROM OLD_TABLE o;
        
    ELSE
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unexpected TG_OP: "%". Should not occur!', TG_OP;
    END CASE;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;
```

**Usage Pattern** (Line 177-179):

```sql
INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
SELECT n.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, NULL, row_to_json(n), remote_ip
FROM NEW_TABLE n;
```

**Note**: This is **statement-level** (not row-level) for bulk efficiency. Triggers are attached to tables via:

```sql
CREATE TRIGGER audit_assignments_insert
    AFTER INSERT ON public.assignments
    REFERENCING NEW TABLE AS NEW_TABLE
    FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION public.audit_statement_trigger();
```

**Audit Table Structure** (Lines 21-31):

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.audit (
    "id" bigint GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY,
    "created_at" timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    "class_id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "user_id" uuid,
    "table" text NOT NULL,
    "old" jsonb,
    "new" jsonb,
    "ip_addr" text,
    PRIMARY KEY (id, created_at)
) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

---

### 8. **Profile + User Role Creation for Enrollment**

**Pattern from admin_create_class** (Lines 80-115):

```sql
-- Create public profile (visible to others)
INSERT INTO public.profiles (
    name,
    class_id,
    is_private_profile
) VALUES (
    (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
    v_class_id,
    false
) RETURNING id INTO v_public_profile_id;

-- Create private profile (personal, only for user)
INSERT INTO public.profiles (
    name,
    class_id,
    is_private_profile
) VALUES (
    (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
    v_class_id,
    true
) RETURNING id INTO v_private_profile_id;

-- Create user role linking both profiles
INSERT INTO public.user_roles (
    user_id,
    class_id,
    role,
    public_profile_id,
    private_profile_id
) VALUES (
    p_created_by,
    v_class_id,
    'admin',
    v_public_profile_id,
    v_private_profile_id
);
```

**Profile Table Definition** (from remote_schema.sql):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "public"."profiles" (
    "id" "uuid" DEFAULT "gen_random_uuid"() NOT NULL,
    "created_at" timestamp with time zone DEFAULT "now"() NOT NULL,
    "name" "text",
    "sis_user_id" "text",
    "time_zone" "text",
    "sortable_name" "text",
    "short_name" "text",
    "avatar_url" "text",
    "class_id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "flair" "text",
    "flair_color" "text",
    "is_private_profile" boolean  -- Added in migrations
);
```

**User Roles Structure** (from admin_portal_system.sql):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "public"."user_roles" (
    "id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "user_id" "uuid" NOT NULL,
    "class_id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "role" "public"."app_role" NOT NULL,
    "canvas_id" numeric,
    "public_profile_id" "uuid" NOT NULL,
    "private_profile_id" "uuid" NOT NULL,
    -- Additional columns added over time:
    "class_section_id" bigint,
    "lab_section_id" bigint,
    "invitation_id" bigint,
    "disabled" boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    "created_at" timestamptz,
    "updated_at" timestamptz,
    "github_org_confirmed" boolean DEFAULT false,
    "invitation_date" timestamp with time zone
);
```

---

### 9. **RLS Policy Example for Admin Access**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` (Lines 1054-1092)

**Pattern** (for invitations table):

```sql
CREATE POLICY "Users can view invitations for classes they belong to" ON "public"."invitations"
  FOR SELECT USING (
    -- Admins can view all invitations
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur
      WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND ur.role = 'admin'
        AND ur.disabled = false
    )
    OR
    -- Non-admins need instructor/grader role in the specific class
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur
      WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND ur.class_id = invitations.class_id
        AND ur.role IN ('instructor', 'grader')
        AND ur.disabled = false
    )
  );
```

**Key Pattern**: 
- Admins get blanket access (no class_id check)
- Regular users need specific role in specific class
- Always check `disabled = false` on user_roles

---

### 10. **User Role Creation from Invitations** 

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20260522120002_invitation_enrollment_functions.sql` (Lines 85-91, simplified)

**Profile Creation Pattern**:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.profiles (name, class_id, is_private_profile)
VALUES (v_public_name, p_class_id, false)
RETURNING id INTO v_public_profile_id;

INSERT INTO public.profiles (name, class_id, is_private_profile)
VALUES (v_display_name, p_class_id, true)
RETURNING id INTO v_private_profile_id;
```

**User Role Insertion**:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.user_roles (
    user_id,
    class_id,
    role,
    public_profile_id,
    private_profile_id,
    invitation_id,
    class_section_id,
    lab_section_id,
    disabled,
    canvas_id
)
SELECT 
    NEW.user_id,
    i.class_id,
    i.role,
    i.public_profile_id,
    i.private_profile_id,
    i.id,
    i.class_section_id,
    i.lab_section_id,
    false,  -- Start as active user
    NEW.sis_user_id::numeric
FROM public.invitations i
WHERE i.sis_user_id = NEW.sis_user_id 
  AND i.status = 'pending'
  AND (i.expires_at IS NULL OR i.expires_at > NOW())
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur 
      WHERE ur.user_id = NEW.user_id AND ur.class_id = i.class_id
  );
```

---

## Summary for Your Implementation

**Use these exact patterns**:

1. **Migration timestamp**: Generate fresh `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (e.g., `20260525120000`)
2. **Authorization**: Always use `authorize_for_admin()` for global admin checks; respect `disabled` column
3. **RPC structure**: 
   - `SECURITY DEFINER`
   - `SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public`
   - Check auth first, validate inputs, then mutate
4. **Profile creation**: Always create **two profiles** (public `is_private_profile=false`, private `is_private_profile=true`)
5. **User roles**: Link instructor/creator to both profiles via `public_profile_id` and `private_profile_id`
6. **Audit**: Automatic via statement-level trigger on tables with `AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE`
7. **RLS policies**: Check `disabled = false` and use `authorize_for_class*` functions
8. **Classes table**: Use `github_org` and `slug` columns for org/prefix data

All patterns are production-tested across ~150+ migrations in this codebase.

```sql
    IF p_term IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Term is required';
    END IF;

    -- Insert new class
    INSERT INTO public.classes (
        name,
        term,
        description,
        github_org,
        slug,
        course_title,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        created_at
    ) VALUES (
        trim(p_name),
        p_term,
        p_description,
        p_github_org_name,
        p_github_template_prefix,
        p_course_title,
        p_start_date,
        p_end_date,
        now()
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_class_id;

    -- Create admin user role for the creator
    -- Create public profile
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (
        name,
        class_id,
        is_private_profile
    ) VALUES (
        (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
        v_class_id,
        false
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_public_profile_id;

    -- Create private profile
    INSERT INTO public.profiles (
        name,
        class_id,
        is_private_profile
    ) VALUES (
        (SELECT name FROM public.users WHERE user_id = p_created_by),
        v_class_id,
        true
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_private_profile_id;

    -- Create admin user role
    INSERT INTO public.user_roles (
        user_id,
        class_id,
        role,
        public_profile_id,
        private_profile_id
    ) VALUES (
        p_created_by,
        v_class_id,
        'admin',
        v_public_profile_id,
        v_private_profile_id
    );

    RETURN v_class_id;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

**Critical Patterns**:
- `SECURITY DEFINER` for privilege escalation
- `SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public` for isolation
- Authorization check FIRST before any mutations
- Create TWO profiles: one public (`is_private_profile = false`), one private (`is_private_profile = true`)
- Both profiles have same `name` and `class_id`
- User role links to both profiles via `public_profile_id` and `private_profile_id`

---

### 4. **admin_get_classes RPC** (Lines 193-241)

**Full SQL**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION admin_get_classes()
RETURNS TABLE (
    id bigint,
    name text,
    term integer,
    description text,
    github_org_name text,
    github_template_prefix text,
    created_at timestamptz,
    student_count bigint,
    instructor_count bigint,
    archived boolean
) AS $$
BEGIN
    SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public;
    
    -- Check admin authorization
    IF NOT authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT 
        c.id,
        c.name,
        c.term,
        c.description,
        c.github_org as github_org_name,
        c.slug as github_template_prefix,
        c.created_at,
        COALESCE(student_counts.student_count, 0) as student_count,
        COALESCE(instructor_counts.instructor_count, 0) as instructor_count,
        COALESCE(c.archived, false) as archived
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT class_id, COUNT(*) as student_count
        FROM public.user_roles
        WHERE role = 'student'
        GROUP BY class_id
    ) student_counts ON c.id = student_counts.class_id
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT class_id, COUNT(*) as instructor_count
        FROM public.user_roles
        WHERE role = 'instructor'
        GROUP BY class_id
    ) instructor_counts ON c.id = instructor_counts.class_id
    ORDER BY c.created_at DESC;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

### 6. **authorizeforclassinstructor Function**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` (Lines 742-770)

**Full Implementation**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION "public"."authorizeforclassinstructor"("class__id" bigint) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE "plpgsql" STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
    SET "search_path" TO ''
    AS $$
begin
    RAISE NOTICE 'authorizeforclassinstructor called with class_id: %, user_id: %', class__id, auth.uid();
    -- First check if user is an admin anywhere - admins have global access
    if exists (
        select 1
        from public.user_roles as r
        where r.user_id = auth.uid() 
          and r.role = 'admin' 
          and r.disabled = false
    ) then
        return true;
    end if;

    -- Otherwise, check for instructor role in this specific class
    return exists (
        select 1
        from public.user_roles as r
        where r.class_id = class__id 
          and r.user_id = auth.uid() 
          and r.role = 'instructor'
          and r.disabled = false
    );
end;
$$;
```

**Key Points**:
- Admins bypass class-specific checks (have global access)
- Checks both admin AND class-specific instructor roles
- Respects `disabled = false` flag
- Uses `SECURITY DEFINER` with `SET search_path = ''` for isolation

### 7. **Audit Log Mechanism**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20251228143943_partitioned_audit_system.sql` (Lines 156-201)

**Statement-Level Trigger Function**:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.audit_statement_trigger()
RETURNS trigger
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = pg_catalog, public, auth
AS $$
DECLARE
    remote_ip text;
    current_user_id uuid;
BEGIN
    -- Get current user ID
    current_user_id := auth.uid();
    
    -- Extract IP address from request headers
    SELECT split_part(
        current_setting('request.headers', true)::json->>'x-forwarded-for',
        ',', 1) INTO remote_ip;
        
    CASE TG_OP
    WHEN 'INSERT' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all new rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT n.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, NULL, row_to_json(n), remote_ip
        FROM NEW_TABLE n;
        
    WHEN 'UPDATE' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all updated rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT COALESCE(n.class_id, o.class_id), current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, 
               row_to_json(o), row_to_json(n), remote_ip
        FROM NEW_TABLE n
        JOIN OLD_TABLE o ON n.id = o.id;
        
    WHEN 'DELETE' THEN
        -- Bulk insert audit records for all deleted rows
        INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
        SELECT o.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, row_to_json(o), NULL, remote_ip
        FROM OLD_TABLE o;
        
    ELSE
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unexpected TG_OP: "%". Should not occur!', TG_OP;
    END CASE;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;
```

**Usage Pattern** (Line 177-179):

```sql
INSERT INTO public.audit (class_id, user_id, "table", old, new, ip_addr)
SELECT n.class_id, current_user_id, TG_TABLE_NAME, NULL, row_to_json(n), remote_ip
FROM NEW_TABLE n;
```

**Note**: This is **statement-level** (not row-level) for bulk efficiency. Triggers are attached to tables via:

```sql
CREATE TRIGGER audit_assignments_insert
    AFTER INSERT ON public.assignments
    REFERENCING NEW TABLE AS NEW_TABLE
    FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION public.audit_statement_trigger();
```

**Audit Table Structure** (Lines 21-31):

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.audit (
    "id" bigint GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY,
    "created_at" timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    "class_id" bigint NOT NULL,
    "user_id" uuid,
    "table" text NOT NULL,
    "old" jsonb,
    "new" jsonb,
    "ip_addr" text,
    PRIMARY KEY (id, created_at)
) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at);
```

### 9. **RLS Policy Example for Admin Access**

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20250820185937_admin_portal_system.sql` (Lines 1054-1092)

**Pattern** (for invitations table):

```sql
CREATE POLICY "Users can view invitations for classes they belong to" ON "public"."invitations"
  FOR SELECT USING (
    -- Admins can view all invitations
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur
      WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND ur.role = 'admin'
        AND ur.disabled = false
    )
    OR
    -- Non-admins need instructor/grader role in the specific class
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur
      WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND ur.class_id = invitations.class_id
        AND ur.role IN ('instructor', 'grader')
        AND ur.disabled = false
    )
  );
```

**Key Pattern**: 
- Admins get blanket access (no class_id check)
- Regular users need specific role in specific class
- Always check `disabled = false` on user_roles

### 10. **User Role Creation from Invitations** 

**File**: `/scratch/platform/supabase/migrations/20260522120002_invitation_enrollment_functions.sql` (Lines 85-91, simplified)

**Profile Creation Pattern**:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.profiles (name, class_id, is_private_profile)
VALUES (v_public_name, p_class_id, false)
RETURNING id INTO v_public_profile_id;

INSERT INTO public.profiles (name, class_id, is_private_profile)
VALUES (v_display_name, p_class_id, true)
RETURNING id INTO v_private_profile_id;
```

**User Role Insertion**:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.user_roles (
    user_id,
    class_id,
    role,
    public_profile_id,
    private_profile_id,
    invitation_id,
    class_section_id,
    lab_section_id,
    disabled,
    canvas_id
)
SELECT 
    NEW.user_id,
    i.class_id,
    i.role,
    i.public_profile_id,
    i.private_profile_id,
    i.id,
    i.class_section_id,
    i.lab_section_id,
    false,  -- Start as active user
    NEW.sis_user_id::numeric
FROM public.invitations i
WHERE i.sis_user_id = NEW.sis_user_id 
  AND i.status = 'pending'
  AND (i.expires_at IS NULL OR i.expires_at > NOW())
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.user_roles ur 
      WHERE ur.user_id = NEW.user_id AND ur.class_id = i.class_id
  );
```

## Summary for Your Implementation

**Use these exact patterns**:

1. **Migration timestamp**: Generate fresh `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (e.g., `20260525120000`)
2. **Authorization**: Always use `authorize_for_admin()` for global admin checks; respect `disabled` column
3. **RPC structure**: 
   - `SECURITY DEFINER`
   - `SET LOCAL search_path = pg_catalog, public`
   - Check auth first, validate inputs, then mutate
4. **Profile creation**: Always create **two profiles** (public `is_private_profile=false`, private `is_private_profile=true`)
5. **User roles**: Link instructor/creator to both profiles via `public_profile_id` and `private_profile_id`
6. **Audit**: Automatic via statement-level trigger on tables with `AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE`
7. **RLS policies**: Check `disabled = false` and use `authorize_for_class*` functions
8. **Classes table**: Use `github_org` and `slug` columns for org/prefix data

All patterns are production-tested across ~150+ migrations in this codebase.

-- GitHub org-level handout/solution template configuration, overridable per class.
--
-- Today the handout/solution template repos are hardcoded constants in the
-- assignment-create-*-repo edge functions:
--   pawtograder/template-assignment-handout
--   pawtograder/template-assignment-grader
-- Institutions that run their own GitHub org need their own templates. This migration
-- adds a github_orgs table (per-org defaults), per-class override columns, a resolution
-- RPC (override -> org default -> hardcoded constant), and admin RPCs for the admin
-- per-org dashboard.

-- The documented fallback defaults, kept in sync with the edge-function constants.
-- Used wherever neither a class override nor an org default is configured.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 1. github_orgs table
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.github_orgs (
    org_name text PRIMARY KEY,
    default_handout_template_repo text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout',
    default_solution_template_repo text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader',
    created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    created_by uuid REFERENCES auth.users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    updated_by uuid REFERENCES auth.users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.github_orgs IS
    'Per-GitHub-org default handout/solution template repos. Keyed by org name (matches classes.github_org).';

-- Keep updated_at fresh on direct updates (RPCs also set it explicitly).
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at_on_github_orgs ON public.github_orgs;
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at_on_github_orgs
    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON public.github_orgs
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE FUNCTION public.set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.github_orgs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Admins (global user_roles.role='admin') have full CRUD.
CREATE POLICY "Admins manage github_orgs" ON public.github_orgs
    FOR ALL
    USING (public.authorize_for_admin())
    WITH CHECK (public.authorize_for_admin());

-- Instructors of any class in the org may read its config.
CREATE POLICY "Instructors view their github_org" ON public.github_orgs
    FOR SELECT
    USING (
        EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM public.user_roles ur
            JOIN public.classes c ON c.id = ur.class_id
            WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
              AND ur.role = 'instructor'
              AND ur.disabled = false
              AND c.github_org = github_orgs.org_name
        )
    );

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 2. Per-class override columns (NULL = inherit org default)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ALTER TABLE public.classes
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS handout_template_repo text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS solution_template_repo text;

COMMENT ON COLUMN public.classes.handout_template_repo IS
    'Per-class override for the handout template repo. NULL inherits the github_orgs default, then the hardcoded constant.';
COMMENT ON COLUMN public.classes.solution_template_repo IS
    'Per-class override for the solution (grader) template repo. NULL inherits the github_orgs default, then the hardcoded constant.';

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 3. Resolution RPC: override -> org default -> hardcoded constant
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.resolve_class_template_repos(p_class_id bigint)
RETURNS TABLE (handout_template_repo text, solution_template_repo text)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Callable by class instructors/admins (UI) and by edge functions (service role).
    IF NOT (auth.role() = 'service_role' OR public.authorizeforclassinstructor(p_class_id)) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: instructor role required for class %', p_class_id;
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        COALESCE(c.handout_template_repo, go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(c.solution_template_repo, go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader')
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = c.github_org
    WHERE c.id = p_class_id;
END;
$$;

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 4. Admin RPCs (global admin only)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- One row per distinct org (union of github_orgs rows and orgs referenced by classes).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_get_github_orgs()
RETURNS TABLE (
    org_name text,
    default_handout_template_repo text,
    default_solution_template_repo text,
    course_count bigint,
    is_configured boolean,
    created_at timestamptz,
    updated_at timestamptz
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH orgs AS (
        SELECT go.org_name FROM public.github_orgs go
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT c.github_org AS org_name FROM public.classes c WHERE c.github_org IS NOT NULL
    )
    SELECT
        o.org_name,
        COALESCE(go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.classes c WHERE c.github_org = o.org_name)::bigint,
        (go.org_name IS NOT NULL) AS is_configured,
        go.created_at,
        go.updated_at
    FROM orgs o
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = o.org_name
    ORDER BY o.org_name;
END;
$$;

-- Create or update an org's default template repos.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_upsert_github_org(
    p_org_name text,
    p_handout text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_solution text DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    IF p_org_name IS NULL OR trim(p_org_name) = '' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Org name is required';
    END IF;

    INSERT INTO public.github_orgs (
        org_name,
        default_handout_template_repo,
        default_solution_template_repo,
        created_by,
        updated_by
    ) VALUES (
        trim(p_org_name),
        COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_handout), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_solution), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        auth.uid(),
        auth.uid()
    )
    ON CONFLICT (org_name) DO UPDATE SET
        default_handout_template_repo = COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_handout), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        default_solution_template_repo = COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_solution), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        updated_by = auth.uid(),
        updated_at = now();
END;
$$;

-- Courses in an org with their override columns and effective (resolved) templates.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_get_org_courses(p_org_name text)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id bigint,
    name text,
    term integer,
    archived boolean,
    handout_template_repo text,
    solution_template_repo text,
    effective_handout_template_repo text,
    effective_solution_template_repo text
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.name,
        c.term,
        COALESCE(c.archived, false),
        c.handout_template_repo,
        c.solution_template_repo,
        COALESCE(c.handout_template_repo, go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(c.solution_template_repo, go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader')
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = c.github_org
    WHERE c.github_org = p_org_name
    ORDER BY c.name;
END;
$$;

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 5. Grants
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.resolve_class_template_repos(bigint) TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_get_github_orgs() TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_upsert_github_org(text, text, text) TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_get_org_courses(text) TO authenticated, service_role;

content.subject: Phase B: Admin per-org dashboard
content.description: Add GitHub Orgs nav link in app/admin/layout.tsx. Create app/admin/github-orgs/{page,layout}.tsx list page and app/admin/github-orgs/[org]/page.tsx detail/editor page using the new admin RPCs. Embed RepoFileEditor for org template repos.

content.subject: Phase C: Monaco RepoFileEditor with commit-back
content.description: Add GitHubWrapper.writeFileToRepo; new repository-write-file edge function; WriteFileRequest type; repositoryWriteFile wrapper in lib/edgeFunctions.ts. Add lib/schemas/github-workflow.schema.json.

content.description: RPC admin_enter_course_as_instructor (idempotent instructor provisioning). Add Manage as instructor actions in AdminDashboardContent + org-course table. Admin-viewing banner via new app/course/[course_id]/manage/layout.tsx.

content.text: `resolve_class_template_repos` is verified across all three precedence scenarios (override > org default > constant) — exactly right. The `admin_*` functions rejected my *simulated* service_role JWT, which just means `authorize_for_admin()` gates on a real admin user, not a raw role string. Since my admin RPCs use the identical guard as the existing working `admin_get_classes`/`admin_create_class`, let me confirm by simulating a real admin user's JWT.

input.command: cd /scratch/platform; CID=$(docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -i 'supabase_db' | head -1); echo "=== actual authorize_for_admin definition ==="; docker exec -i "$CID" psql -U postgres -d postgres -c "SELECT pg_get_functiondef('public.authorize_for_admin'::regproc);" 2>&1 | sed -n '1,30p'

content.content:
=== actual authorize_for_admin definition ===
                                    pg_get_functiondef                                    
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.authorize_for_admin(p_user_id uuid DEFAULT auth.uid())+
  RETURNS boolean                                                                        +
  LANGUAGE plpgsql                                                                       +
  STABLE SECURITY DEFINER                                                                +
  SET search_path TO ''                                                                  +
 AS $function$                                                                           +
 BEGIN                                                                                   +
     -- Allow service role (for edge functions)                                          +
     IF auth.role() = 'service_role' THEN                                                +
         RETURN true;                                                                    +
     END IF;                                                                             +
                                                                                         +
     -- Optimized admin check using SQL                                                  +
     RETURN EXISTS (                                                                     +
         SELECT 1                                                                        +
         FROM public.user_roles ur                                                       +
         WHERE ur.user_id = p_user_id                                                    +
           AND ur.role = 'admin'                                                         +
           AND ur.disabled = false                                                       +
     );                                                                                  +
 END;                                                                                    +
 $function$                                                                              +
 
(1 row)

content.text: Important: the only instructor UPDATE policy on `classes` is column-scoped to calendar/discord ids (`only_calendar_or_discord_ids_changed`). A "plain useUpdate" setting template columns would be **rejected by RLS**. This is a necessary deviation from the plan — I'll add a `SECURITY DEFINER` RPC guarded by `authorizeforclassinstructor` instead (also matches the repo's "prefer RPCs" convention). Adding it to the migration:

input.new_string:
-- Instructor-facing setter for the per-class overrides.
-- A SECURITY DEFINER RPC is required because the only instructor UPDATE policy on
-- public.classes is column-scoped (only_calendar_or_discord_ids_changed), so a direct
-- UPDATE of the template columns would be rejected by RLS. Guarded by
-- authorizeforclassinstructor (admins pass too). Pass NULL/empty to clear an override.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.set_class_template_overrides(
    p_class_id bigint,
    p_handout text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_solution text DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT (auth.role() = 'service_role' OR public.authorizeforclassinstructor(p_class_id)) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: instructor role required for class %', p_class_id;
    END IF;

    UPDATE public.classes
    SET handout_template_repo = NULLIF(trim(p_handout), ''),
        solution_template_repo = NULLIF(trim(p_solution), '')
    WHERE id = p_class_id;
END;
$$;

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 4. Admin RPCs (global admin only)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

toolUseResult.originalFile:
-- GitHub org-level handout/solution template configuration, overridable per class.
--
-- Today the handout/solution template repos are hardcoded constants in the
-- assignment-create-*-repo edge functions:
--   pawtograder/template-assignment-handout
--   pawtograder/template-assignment-grader
-- Institutions that run their own GitHub org need their own templates. This migration
-- adds a github_orgs table (per-org defaults), per-class override columns, a resolution
-- RPC (override -> org default -> hardcoded constant), and admin RPCs for the admin
-- per-org dashboard.

-- The documented fallback defaults, kept in sync with the edge-function constants.
-- Used wherever neither a class override nor an org default is configured.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 1. github_orgs table
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.github_orgs (
    org_name text PRIMARY KEY,
    default_handout_template_repo text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout',
    default_solution_template_repo text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader',
    created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    created_by uuid REFERENCES auth.users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    updated_by uuid REFERENCES auth.users (id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);

COMMENT ON TABLE public.github_orgs IS
    'Per-GitHub-org default handout/solution template repos. Keyed by org name (matches classes.github_org).';

-- Keep updated_at fresh on direct updates (RPCs also set it explicitly).
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at_on_github_orgs ON public.github_orgs;
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at_on_github_orgs
    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON public.github_orgs
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE FUNCTION public.set_updated_at();

ALTER TABLE public.github_orgs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Admins (global user_roles.role='admin') have full CRUD.
CREATE POLICY "Admins manage github_orgs" ON public.github_orgs
    FOR ALL
    USING (public.authorize_for_admin())
    WITH CHECK (public.authorize_for_admin());

-- Instructors of any class in the org may read its config.
CREATE POLICY "Instructors view their github_org" ON public.github_orgs
    FOR SELECT
    USING (
        EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM public.user_roles ur
            JOIN public.classes c ON c.id = ur.class_id
            WHERE ur.user_id = auth.uid()
              AND ur.role = 'instructor'
              AND ur.disabled = false
              AND c.github_org = github_orgs.org_name
        )
    );

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 2. Per-class override columns (NULL = inherit org default)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ALTER TABLE public.classes
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS handout_template_repo text,
    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS solution_template_repo text;

COMMENT ON COLUMN public.classes.handout_template_repo IS
    'Per-class override for the handout template repo. NULL inherits the github_orgs default, then the hardcoded constant.';
COMMENT ON COLUMN public.classes.solution_template_repo IS
    'Per-class override for the solution (grader) template repo. NULL inherits the github_orgs default, then the hardcoded constant.';

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 3. Resolution RPC: override -> org default -> hardcoded constant
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.resolve_class_template_repos(p_class_id bigint)
RETURNS TABLE (handout_template_repo text, solution_template_repo text)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Callable by class instructors/admins (UI) and by edge functions (service role).
    IF NOT (auth.role() = 'service_role' OR public.authorizeforclassinstructor(p_class_id)) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: instructor role required for class %', p_class_id;
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        COALESCE(c.handout_template_repo, go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(c.solution_template_repo, go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader')
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = c.github_org
    WHERE c.id = p_class_id;
END;
$$;

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 4. Admin RPCs (global admin only)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- One row per distinct org (union of github_orgs rows and orgs referenced by classes).
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_get_github_orgs()
RETURNS TABLE (
    org_name text,
    default_handout_template_repo text,
    default_solution_template_repo text,
    course_count bigint,
    is_configured boolean,
    created_at timestamptz,
    updated_at timestamptz
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH orgs AS (
        SELECT go.org_name FROM public.github_orgs go
        UNION
        SELECT DISTINCT c.github_org AS org_name FROM public.classes c WHERE c.github_org IS NOT NULL
    )
    SELECT
        o.org_name,
        COALESCE(go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.classes c WHERE c.github_org = o.org_name)::bigint,
        (go.org_name IS NOT NULL) AS is_configured,
        go.created_at,
        go.updated_at
    FROM orgs o
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = o.org_name
    ORDER BY o.org_name;
END;
$$;

-- Create or update an org's default template repos.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_upsert_github_org(
    p_org_name text,
    p_handout text DEFAULT NULL,
    p_solution text DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    IF p_org_name IS NULL OR trim(p_org_name) = '' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Org name is required';
    END IF;

    INSERT INTO public.github_orgs (
        org_name,
        default_handout_template_repo,
        default_solution_template_repo,
        created_by,
        updated_by
    ) VALUES (
        trim(p_org_name),
        COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_handout), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_solution), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        auth.uid(),
        auth.uid()
    )
    ON CONFLICT (org_name) DO UPDATE SET
        default_handout_template_repo = COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_handout), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        default_solution_template_repo = COALESCE(NULLIF(trim(p_solution), ''), 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader'),
        updated_by = auth.uid(),
        updated_at = now();
END;
$$;

-- Courses in an org with their override columns and effective (resolved) templates.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.admin_get_org_courses(p_org_name text)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id bigint,
    name text,
    term integer,
    archived boolean,
    handout_template_repo text,
    solution_template_repo text,
    effective_handout_template_repo text,
    effective_solution_template_repo text
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = ''
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF NOT public.authorize_for_admin() THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: Admin role required';
    END IF;

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.name,
        c.term,
        COALESCE(c.archived, false),
        c.handout_template_repo,
        c.solution_template_repo,
        COALESCE(c.handout_template_repo, go.default_handout_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-handout'),
        COALESCE(c.solution_template_repo, go.default_solution_template_repo, 'pawtograder/template-assignment-grader')
    FROM public.classes c
    LEFT JOIN public.github_orgs go ON go.org_name = c.github_org
    WHERE c.github_org = p_org_name
    ORDER BY c.name;
END;
$$;

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 5. Grants
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.resolve_class_template_repos(bigint) TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_get_github_orgs() TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_upsert_github_org(text, text, text) TO authenticated, service_role;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.admin_get_org_courses(text) TO authenticated, service_role;

+-- A SECURITY DEFINER RPC is required because the only instructor UPDATE policy on
+-- public.classes is column-scoped (only_calendar_or_discord_ids_changed), so a direct
+-- UPDATE of the template columns would be rejected by RLS. Guarded by
+-- authorizeforclassinstructor (admins pass too). Pass NULL/empty to clear an override.
+        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Access denied: instructor role required for class %', p_class_id;

+    -- Idempotent: if the admin already has an active role in this class, do nothing.
+        WHERE ur.user_id = v_user_id AND ur.class_id = p_class_id AND ur.disabled = false
+    SELECT u.name INTO v_name FROM public.users u WHERE u.user_id = v_user_id;
+    -- Public + private profiles, mirroring admin_create_class / enrollment helpers.

19	  const hasErrors = errors.length > 0;

56	              </Icon>
57	            </Flex>
58	          </Card.Header>
59	          <Card.Body>
60	            <Text fontSize="2xl" fontWeight="bold">
61	              {formatCount(totalUsers)}
62	            </Text>
63	            <Text fontSize="xs" color="gray.500">
64	              Registered users
65	            </Text>
66	          </Card.Body>
67	        </Card.Root>
68	
69	        <Card.Root>
70	          <Card.Header>
71	            <Flex justify="space-between" align="center">
72	              <Text fontSize="sm" fontWeight="medium">
73	                Total Enrollments

75	              <Icon color="gray.500">
76	                <FileText size={16} />
77	              </Icon>
78	            </Flex>

81	            <Text fontSize="2xl" fontWeight="bold">
82	              {formatCount(totalEnrollments)}
83	            </Text>

85	              User enrollments

103	              {hasErrors ? "Degraded" : "Healthy"}
104	            </Text>

106	              {hasErrors ? "Some admin metrics are currently unavailable" : "All systems operational"}
107	            </Text>
